page: OVHcloud b2-15 is a general-purpose virtual server hosted on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor configuration lacks hyperthreading, providing a 1:1 ratio of threads to physical cores. It is equipped with 15.0 GB of system memory, yielding 3.75 GB per core, and includes 100 GB of local SSD storage. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the b2-15 sits primarily in the lower tiers for compute-heavy tasks. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place the instance in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested hardware. However, memory subsystem performance is more balanced; small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth reaches 347.69 GB/sec, placing it in the middle 50% of benchmarks. Large-block uncached memory read and write speeds are weaker, registering at 37.17 GB/sec and 22.32 GB/sec respectively.

From a workload perspective, the instance is suited for moderate, non-compute-intensive applications. Database operations and Redis SET benchmarks show average performance, with Redis achieving 5.93 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is relatively low at 4.56 GB/sec. The instance can handle light language model tasks, processing 424.01 tokens per second on a small 135M model, but struggles significantly with larger 7B models. This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient entry point for development environments, basic web serving, and lightweight database tasks where high-performance compute is not a primary requirement.
description: OVHcloud b2-15 is a general-purpose virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Haswell processor. It is configured with 4 dedicated physical cores (4 vCPUs) running at 2.0 GHz, 15.0 GB of RAM, and 100 GB of local SSD storage. Lacking hyperthreading and GPU acceleration, this instance delivers modest compute capabilities, with benchmarks placing its single-core and multi-core CPU performance in the lower 10% to 25% tier. However, it maintains average performance in memory bandwidth for small cached blocks and database operations, achieving 5.93 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. The b2-15 represents a qualitatively cost-effective option for workloads that do not demand high-performance compute, making it suitable for basic web serving, development environments, and lightweight database applications.
og_description: OVHcloud b2-15 is a general-purpose KVM virtual server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Haswell vCPUs, 15 GB of RAM, and 100 GB of local SSD storage, designed for basic workloads and development environments.
meta_description: OVHcloud b2-15 is a general-purpose KVM virtual server with 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 15 GB RAM, and 100 GB local SSD storage for light workloads.
tagline: A dedicated four-core virtual server with balanced memory and local SSD storage designed for entry-level workloads and development environments.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated x86_64 Intel Haswell vCPUs at 2.0 GHz without hyperthreading
15.0 GB of system memory and 100 GB of local SSD storage
Average performance in small-block cached memory bandwidth and Redis operations
Includes one complimentary public IPv4 address
Best suited for lightweight databases, basic web serving, and development environments
